mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive returns occur in less than 50% of rate-cut months.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that immediately suppress consumer spending expectations and drive negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in more than 65% of triggered months or the signal loses statistical significance with a p-value above 0.10 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in real personal income and housing starts indicate synchronized consumer and construction demand expansion, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over a three-month horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative returns in more than 55% of occurrences or the interaction term fails to improve model fit over additive effects with an F-test p-value above 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices during early-cycle expansions reflect capacity utilization improvements that boost corporate earnings revisions, yielding positive S&P 500 returns over a one-week horizon.
failure_condition: The signal flips to negative returns during early-cycle expansions or shows no predictive power with a Rank IC below 0.01 when filtered by NBER expansion phases.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP directly correlates with aggregate corporate revenue expansion, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon following each data release.
failure_condition: Forward returns are negative in more than 60% of quarters with positive GDP growth or the signal decays to zero predictive accuracy within 10 trading days.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly inflation reading above 0.35% triggers expectations of aggressive Federal Reserve tightening, compressing equity valuations and driving negative S&P 500 returns over a three-month horizon.
failure_condition: Returns remain positive in more than 58% of high-inflation months or the threshold effect vanishes when controlling for forward rate expectations.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in total nonfarm payrolls and decreases in the unemployment rate confirm robust job creation that lifts consumer confidence and equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields negative returns in more than 50% of months or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero with a p-value above 0.10 in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Declining housing starts during late-cycle expansions signal weakening consumer leverage capacity and impending demand contraction, producing negative S&P 500 returns over a one-week horizon.
failure_condition: The signal produces positive returns in more than 60% of late-cycle decline months or loses predictive significance when filtered by credit cycle indicators.
